title: cracca dasycarpa
intro: Cracca dasycarpa, also known as the "hairy broom" or "hairy broom senna", is a plant species native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities. The health benefits of Cracca dasycarpa are attributed to its ability to reduce fever, alleviate symptoms of colds and flu, and soothe digestive issues due to its anthraquinone content. Herbal preparations of Cracca dasycarpa include teas and infusions, which can be made by steeping the dried leaves and roots in hot water.
